LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JAMES DURYEA III'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS AGAINST STEVEN W. STRAIN PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 473(B). THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING. GRANTED. USING AN AFFIDAVIT OF FAULT FROM ATTORNEY STEVEN STRAIN, DEFENDANTS SECURED RELIEF FROM DEFAULTS UNDER CCP 473. WHEN THAT HAPPENS, CCP 473 SAYS I "SHALL" REQUIRE THE ATTORNEY (HERE STRAIN) "TO PAY REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES AND COSTS TO OPPOSING COUNSEL OR PARTIES." PLAINTIFF SO MOVES. STRAIN FILED NO OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION. DEFENDANTS DID FILE AN OPPOSITION, BUT DO NOT SUGGEST HOW THEY COULD HAVE STANDING TO OPPOSE AN OBLIGATION THAT IS STRAIN'S, NOT THEIRS. NOR AM I AWARE OF ANY AUTHORITY FOR SUCH STANDING. IN ANY EVENT, I FIND THE REQUESTED FEES AND COSTS REASONABLE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: REYES HUNTER, CSR #6576

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ANTHONY BACIGALUPI AND HEMP20'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THE MOTION IS TIMELY AND PROVIDES SUFFICIENT SUPPORT FOR BOTH MANDATORY AND DISCRETIONARY RELIEF PER CCP 473. MOREOVER, CALIFORNIA'S STRONG PUBLIC POLICY OF DECIDING CASES ON THEIR MERITS FAVORS THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. PLAINTIFF JAMES DURYEA III'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE IS DENIED. THERE IS AMPLE BASIS TO GRANT THE MOTION AND ALLOWING DISCOVERY ON THE MOTION WILL ONLY INCREASE THE COST OF LITIGATING THIS CASE AND DIVERT THE PARTIES' FOCUS FROM THE MERITS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Set For Case Management Conference On Sep-06-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Aug-17-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Against Steven W. Strain Pursuant To C.C.P. 473(B)
Off Calendar Aug-09-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF JAMES DURYEA III'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Against Steven W. Strain Pursuant To C.C.P. 473(B) is GRANTED. Using an affidavit of fault from attorney Steven Strain, defendants secured relief from defaults under CCP 473. When that happens, CCP 473 says I "shall" require the attorney (here Strain) "to pay reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs to opposing counsel or parties." Plaintiff so moves. Strain filed no opposition to the motion. Defendants did file an opposition, but do not suggest how they could have standing to oppose an obligation that is Strain's, not theirs. Nor am I aware of any authority for such standing. In any event, I find the requested fees and costs reasonable. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, Bus. Tel: 530-339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Pursuant To Ccp 473(B)
Off Calendar Jun-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No apperances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF JAMES DURYEA III'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs Pursuant To CCP 473(B) against Steven Strain, defendants' former counsel, is denied without prejudice to being refiled. Because Mr. Strain is neither a party to this action nor has he appeared as counsel for a party to this action and this motion effectively seeks sanctions against him for his neglect or mistake, due process requires that the notice of motion make clear to Mr. Strain that the motion is against him. (See Weil and Brown book at 9:1092 et seq.) However, the notice of motion is addressed "To all parties and their attorneys of record" and does not state that any relief is sought against Mr. Strain. No opposition filed by Mr. Strain. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (D302)

Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Kreft of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP telephone number 415-421-3400 for Defendants Anthony Bacigalupi and HEMP20.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ANTHONY BACIGALUPI and HEMP20's Motion For Relief From Default is granted. The motion is timely and provides sufficient support for both mandatory and discretionary relief per CCP 473. Moreover, California's strong public policy of deciding cases on their merits favors the granting of the motion. Plaintiff James Duryea III's request for a continuance is denied. There is ample basis to grant the motion and allowing discovery on the motion will only increase the cost of litigating this case and divert the parties' focus from the merits. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/HK)
